title: eriogonum wrightii subsp. pringlei
intro: Eriogonum wrightii subsp. pringlei, also known as Pringle's Mormon tea, is a shrub plant native to the southwestern United States. This plant is used medicinally due to its expectorant properties, which can help relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that may contribute to its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can aid in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Eriogonum wrightii subsp. pringlei is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for consumption, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for use as a medicinal treatment.
